_comment_root: Pyright configuration. We START with `basic` mode across the whole `app/` package — the codebase has been Python-typed but never machine-checked, so flipping straight to `strict` would surface hundreds of issues and block CI on the first run. The plan is to ratchet up by directory: core/ + services/ to `strict` first (smallest surface, highest leverage); workers/ + api/ later once the easy wins are in. Tests stay basic — over-typing fixtures fights pytest's magic.
_comment_module_strictness: Per-directory ratchet. Add a path here when its files are clean under stricter rules.

executionEnvironments._strictness: Strict mode: smallest surface (security, logger, middleware, secrets, rate_limit, metrics). Any new code here MUST type-check strictly so the foundation can be trusted.

_comment_global_rules: Rules applied to everything else (basic mode). Mostly defaults; we explicitly downgrade a handful of noisy checks that fire heavily on SQLAlchemy 2 + Pydantic v2 patterns.
